nouns.summary: Jacob is described as having fathered (ἔγεννήσεν) Joseph.
Joseph is stated to be the son born from Jacob’s fathering, and he is identified as the man/husband associated with Mary.
The text states that Joseph is “the man” (ἄνδρα) of Mary, identifying him in relation to her.
Mary is described as the person from whom Jesus was born (ἐξ ἧς ἐγεννήθη Ἰησοῦς).
Jesus is said to have been born from Mary.
Jesus is identified as “the one called Christ” (ὁ λεγόμενος Χριστός).
